current.description: Critical authentication and database connection failures causing complete system unavailability
current.why: Multiple 500 errors across core endpoints (/health, /api/me, /api/employees) indicate fundamental infrastructure failure. Without stable auth and database connectivity, ALL features (clock-in, scheduling, payroll) are completely blocked. This is a Severity 1 outage.
unblocks: Users can successfully authenticate and access the application
Clock-in/out functionality becomes available
Employee scheduling and management features become usable
Task assignment system can function
Payroll reports can be generated
All downstream features dependent on working auth and database
evidenceFromCrawl: GET /health returned 500 - Application health check failing
GET /api/me returned 500 - User authentication endpoint failing
GET /api/employees returned 500 - Employee data retrieval failing
Multiple unhandled promise rejections in server logs
Database connection errors preventing all data operations
current.goal: Reliability of LIVE features — Clock-in/out, geofencing, scheduling, payroll reports, task assignment, and Clerk auth MUST work flawlessly

steps.description: Add comprehensive error handling and connection retry logic to database initialization. Implement connection pooling configuration, timeout handling, and graceful degradation. Add structured logging for connection events.
successCriteria: Database connection established successfully on server start
Connection errors logged with structured context
Retry logic attempts reconnection with exponential backoff
Connection pool metrics logged at startup

steps.description: Implement proper health check endpoint with database connectivity test, Clerk auth service check, and graceful error responses. Return 503 (Service Unavailable) with detailed error info when dependencies fail instead of 500.
successCriteria: Health endpoint returns 200 when all services are healthy
Health endpoint returns 503 with structured error when database is down
Health endpoint includes response time metrics
No unhandled promise rejections in health check

steps.description: Add comprehensive error handling to Clerk authentication middleware. Catch and properly handle ClerkAPIError, network timeouts, and invalid tokens. Return 401 for auth failures, 503 for Clerk service unavailable.
successCriteria: Authentication failures return proper 401 responses
Clerk service failures return 503 with retry-after header
All auth errors are caught and logged with context
No unhandled promise rejections in auth middleware

steps.description: Wrap /api/me endpoint in try/catch with proper error handling. Handle database query failures, Clerk user lookup failures, and return appropriate HTTP status codes with structured error responses.
successCriteria: Endpoint returns 200 with user data on success
Database errors return 503 with structured error
Auth errors return 401 with clear message
All promises properly awaited and errors caught

steps.description: Add comprehensive error handling to employees endpoint. Wrap database queries in try/catch, handle RBAC permission failures, add input validation with Zod, return proper status codes (200, 403, 503).
successCriteria: Successful queries return 200 with employee data
Permission failures return 403 with clear message
Database failures return 503 with structured error
All database queries use parameterized inputs via Drizzle

steps.description: Add global error handler middleware for Express, uncaughtException handler, unhandledRejection handler. Log all errors with structured context. Ensure server stays alive on non-fatal errors.
successCriteria: Global error handler catches all unhandled Express errors
Process stays alive on handled errors
Uncaught exceptions logged with full stack trace
Unhandled promise rejections logged and don't crash server

steps.description: Create structured logging utility to replace console.log. Support log levels (debug, info, warn, error), add context fields (requestId, userId, endpoint), integrate with error tracking.
successCriteria: Logger exports debug, info, warn, error functions
All log entries include timestamp and level
Error logs include stack traces
Logger is imported and used in at least one fixed file

steps.description: Extract database connection logic into dedicated module with connection pooling config, health check function, graceful shutdown handler. Export singleton connection instance.
successCriteria: Connection pool properly configured with min/max connections
Health check function tests database connectivity
Graceful shutdown closes connections on SIGTERM
Module exports stable singleton instance

current.completionCriteria: All health check endpoints return 200 status. /api/me and /api/employees return successful responses for authenticated users. Zero unhandled promise rejections in server logs for 5 minutes of continuous operation. Database connection remains stable across 100 consecutive API requests.

history.description: Critical authentication and routing infrastructure failures causing 401/404 errors across the entire application
history.why: Authentication middleware is failing or misconfigured, causing legitimate user requests to be rejected. Multiple core routes return 404, indicating missing or incorrectly mounted route handlers. This blocks ALL user workflows — employees can't clock in, managers can't view schedules, owners can't access reports. This is a foundational infrastructure failure.
unblocks: Clock-in/out functionality becomes accessible to authenticated users
Schedule management and viewing becomes possible
Task assignment and tracking can function
Payroll report generation becomes available
Activity logging and audit trails work correctly
All dashboard and admin functionality becomes usable
evidenceFromCrawl: GET /api/auth/session → 401 (auth check failing for legitimate users)
GET /api/employees → 404 (employee management completely unavailable)
GET /api/time-entries → 404 (clock-in/out data inaccessible)
GET /api/schedules → 404 (scheduling system unreachable)
GET /api/tasks → 404 (task management broken)
GET /api/payroll/periods → 404 (payroll reports unavailable)
GET /api/activity-log → 404 (audit trail not working)
Multiple dashboard pages return empty/error states due to failed API calls
history.goal: Reliability of LIVE features — Clock-in/out, geofencing, scheduling, payroll reports, task assignment, and Clerk auth MUST work flawlessly

steps.description: Verify Express route mounting order and middleware chain. Ensure Clerk auth middleware is correctly configured and routes are mounted in proper order (health check first, auth middleware, then protected routes). Check that all route handlers are actually imported and mounted.
successCriteria: All route imports are present and not undefined
Clerk middleware is applied before protected routes
Route mounting order follows: health → auth → api routes
No route path conflicts or duplicates

steps.description: Fix /api/auth/session endpoint to properly return Clerk session data. Ensure it handles both authenticated and unauthenticated states gracefully with proper status codes and error messages.
successCriteria: Returns 200 with session data when authenticated
Returns 401 with clear error message when not authenticated
Includes proper TypeScript types for session response
Has try/catch error handling

steps.description: Ensure employees route handler exists, is properly exported, and implements GET /api/employees with Clerk RBAC checks. Add proper error handling and pagination.
successCriteria: Route responds with 200 and employee list for authorized users
Returns 403 for users without employee:read permission
Includes pagination parameters (limit, offset)
Has Zod input validation for query params
Uses Drizzle ORM for database queries

steps.description: Create or fix time-entries route handler with GET endpoint that returns time entries for authorized users. Must include proper RBAC checks (employees see own entries, managers see all).
successCriteria: Returns 200 with time entries array
Filters by employee ID for non-manager roles
Includes date range filtering
Has proper error handling and logging
Uses parameterized Drizzle queries

steps.description: Create or fix schedules route handler with GET endpoint. Must support date range queries and respect RBAC (employees see own schedule, managers see all).
successCriteria: Returns 200 with schedule data
Filters by date range query params
Respects role-based access control
Includes related shift and employee data
Has Zod validation for query parameters

steps.description: Create or fix tasks route handler with GET endpoint. Must support filtering by assignee and status, with proper RBAC enforcement.
successCriteria: Returns 200 with tasks array
Supports filtering by assignee, status, priority
Employees see own tasks, managers see all tasks
Has proper error handling
Uses Drizzle ORM with pagination

steps.description: Create or fix payroll route handler with GET /api/payroll/periods endpoint. Must calculate pay periods and return payroll data with proper manager-only access control.
successCriteria: Returns 200 with payroll periods array
Restricts access to Owner/Admin roles only
Includes accurate time entry aggregation
Uses decimal arithmetic for pay calculations
Has comprehensive error handling

steps.description: Create or fix activity-log route handler with GET endpoint. Must return audit trail events with proper admin-only access control and pagination.
successCriteria: Returns 200 with activity log entries
Restricts to Admin/Owner roles
Supports pagination and date filtering
Includes actor and action details
Has proper error handling

history.completionCriteria: All API endpoints return proper responses (200 for authorized requests, 401 for unauthenticated, 403 for unauthorized). Crawl shows zero 404 errors on API routes and zero 401 errors for authenticated test user. All dashboard pages load successfully with data.

history.description: Critical authentication and database connection failures are blocking ALL app functionality. Clerk auth integration is misconfigured causing 401 errors across the application, and database queries are failing with connection errors.
history.why: Authentication is the gateway to everything. If users can't authenticate, they can't clock in, view schedules, complete tasks, or access any feature. Database connection failures compound this by preventing any data operations even if auth succeeds. This is a total blocker affecting 100% of user workflows.
unblocks: Users can successfully log in and access the application
Clock-in/out functionality becomes accessible
Schedule viewing and task management becomes operational
Payroll reports can be generated
All API endpoints can serve authenticated requests
Database queries can execute successfully
evidenceFromCrawl: 401 Unauthorized errors on /api/auth/session endpoint
Multiple pages failing to load due to auth failures
Database connection timeouts in server logs

Users unable to access protected routes
history.goal: Reliability of LIVE features — Clock-in/out, geofencing, scheduling, payroll reports, task assignment, and Clerk auth MUST work flawlessly

successCriteria: Clerk middleware initializes without errors on server startup
Auth middleware correctly validates Clerk session tokens
401 responses include proper error messages for debugging

successCriteria: Database connection establishes successfully on server startup
Connection pool maintains healthy connections

history.completionCriteria: All pages load successfully with proper authentication. Health check endpoint returns 200 with all subsystems operational. Users can log in, access protected routes, and perform database operations without 401 or 500 errors. Zero authentication or database connection errors in server logs for 5 consecutive minutes.

history.description: Geofencing validation is completely broken — employees can clock in from anywhere, violating legal time-tracking compliance requirements
history.why: The geofence check in clock-in endpoint returns success even when location validation fails. This is a compliance risk (wage/hour laws require accurate location tracking) and blocks trust in the entire time tracking system. Without reliable geofencing, payroll data is legally questionable.
unblocks: Legal compliance for time tracking
Trust in payroll accuracy
Ability to enforce on-site work requirements
Foundation for future geo-based features (shift reminders, proximity alerts)
Manager confidence in reported work hours
evidenceFromCrawl: POST /api/time-entries/clock-in returns 200 success even when user is 5000+ meters from store location
Geofence validation function exists but result is ignored in the route handler
No error logging when geofence check fails
Database allows time_entries to be created without location verification
Employee can clock in from home, work 8 hours at store, clock out from home — system accepts all events
history.goal: Reliability of LIVE features — Clock-in/out, geofencing, scheduling, payroll reports, task assignment, and Clerk auth MUST work flawlessly

steps.description: Fix clock-in route to REJECT requests when geofence validation fails. Change from logging warning to returning 403 error with clear message. Add structured error logging.
successCriteria: POST /api/time-entries/clock-in returns 403 when user is >100m from store
Error response includes distance and required radius
Successful clock-in still works when within geofence

steps.description: Apply same geofence enforcement to clock-out endpoint. Currently clock-out has no location validation at all.
successCriteria: POST /api/time-entries/clock-out returns 403 when user is >100m from store
Error response matches clock-in format
Successful clock-out works within geofence

steps.description: Add input validation to Haversine distance function — reject null/undefined coordinates with clear error instead of returning NaN. Log when validation is called with invalid inputs.
successCriteria: Function throws error with descriptive message when coords are invalid
Invalid coord attempts are logged with context
Valid coords still calculate distance correctly

steps.description: Create reusable geofence validation middleware that can be applied to clock-in/clock-out routes. Centralizes validation logic and ensures consistent error responses.
successCriteria: Middleware extracts lat/lng from request body
Calls Haversine with store location from env
Returns 403 with structured error if distance > threshold
Calls next() if validation passes

steps.description: Refactor clock-in and clock-out routes to use the new geofence validation middleware. Remove inline validation code.
successCriteria: Both routes use middleware
No duplicate validation logic
Error responses are consistent
Tests pass

steps.description: Add database constraint: time_entries table should require latitude/longitude fields (make NOT NULL). Prevents storing entries without location data.
successCriteria: Migration file created
latitude and longitude columns set to NOT NULL
Existing entries backfilled with placeholder or deleted if invalid

history.completionCriteria: Employee attempting to clock in from >100m away receives 403 error and cannot create time entry. Crawl shows 0 successful clock-in attempts from invalid locations. Payroll report only includes entries with verified geofence compliance.

history.description: Critical authentication and authorization failures blocking all protected routes. Multiple 401/403 errors across core features (clock-in, schedule management, payroll, tasks) prevent users from accessing the app's primary functionality.
history.why: Auth is the foundation layer. When auth fails, EVERYTHING fails. Users can't clock in (blocking workforce management), can't view schedules (blocking shift management), can't access payroll (blocking financial operations), and can't manage tasks (blocking daily operations). This is the single point of failure for the entire application.
unblocks: Clock-in/out functionality becomes accessible
Schedule viewing and shift management becomes operational
Payroll reports become accessible to authorized users
Task assignment and tracking becomes functional
All downstream LIVE features that depend on authenticated requests
User onboarding and member directory access
Activity logging and audit trail functionality

GET /api/members → 401 Unauthorized (member directory blocked)
GET /api/time-entries/current → 401 Unauthorized (clock-in status check failing)

GET /api/schedules/current → 401 Unauthorized (schedule viewing blocked)
GET /api/tasks → 401 Unauthorized (task management blocked)
GET /api/payroll/current-period → 401 Unauthorized (payroll access blocked)
Pattern: 100% of protected API routes returning 401/403 errors
history.goal: Reliability of LIVE features — Clock-in/out, geofencing, scheduling, payroll reports, task assignment, and Clerk auth MUST work flawlessly

steps.description: Fix /api/employees/me endpoint to return current user profile using authenticated userId from middleware.
successCriteria: Endpoint returns profile for authenticated user
Response includes role and permissions
No 401 errors for valid sessions
Profile data matches database employee record

steps.description: Ensure task routes properly authenticate and filter tasks by user permissions. Employees see assigned tasks, managers see all tasks.
successCriteria: Task list loads for authenticated users
Tasks filtered by role permissions
Employees see only assigned tasks
Managers see all tasks

steps.description: Verify auth middleware is correctly registered in Express app before protected routes. Check middleware order and ensure Clerk SDK is properly initialized.
successCriteria: Auth middleware registered globally or per-route correctly
Middleware order allows auth before route handlers
Clerk SDK initialized with correct environment variables
No middleware conflicts or overrides

history.completionCriteria: All protected API endpoints return 200 OK for authenticated users. No 401 Unauthorized errors in crawl results. Clock-in, schedule view, task list, and profile endpoints all functional.

history.description: Critical authentication and database connection failures causing complete system unavailability
history.why: Multiple 500 errors across core endpoints (/health, /api/me, /api/employees) indicate fundamental infrastructure failure. Without stable auth and database connectivity, ALL features (clock-in, scheduling, payroll) are completely blocked. This is a Severity 1 outage.
unblocks: Users can successfully authenticate and access the application
Clock-in/out functionality becomes available
Employee scheduling and management features become usable
Task assignment system can function
Payroll reports can be generated
All downstream features dependent on working auth and database
evidenceFromCrawl: GET /health returned 500 - Application health check failing
GET /api/me returned 500 - User authentication endpoint failing
GET /api/employees returned 500 - Employee data retrieval failing
Multiple unhandled promise rejections in server logs
Database connection errors preventing all data operations
history.goal: Reliability of LIVE features — Clock-in/out, geofencing, scheduling, payroll reports, task assignment, and Clerk auth MUST work flawlessly

steps.description: Add comprehensive error handling and connection retry logic to database initialization. Implement connection pooling configuration, timeout handling, and graceful degradation. Add structured logging for connection events.
successCriteria: Database connection established successfully on server start
Connection errors logged with structured context
Retry logic attempts reconnection with exponential backoff
Connection pool metrics logged at startup

steps.description: Implement proper health check endpoint with database connectivity test, Clerk auth service check, and graceful error responses. Return 503 (Service Unavailable) with detailed error info when dependencies fail instead of 500.
successCriteria: Health endpoint returns 200 when all services are healthy
Health endpoint returns 503 with structured error when database is down
Health endpoint includes response time metrics
No unhandled promise rejections in health check

steps.description: Add comprehensive error handling to Clerk authentication middleware. Catch and properly handle ClerkAPIError, network timeouts, and invalid tokens. Return 401 for auth failures, 503 for Clerk service unavailable.
successCriteria: Authentication failures return proper 401 responses
Clerk service failures return 503 with retry-after header
All auth errors are caught and logged with context
No unhandled promise rejections in auth middleware

steps.description: Wrap /api/me endpoint in try/catch with proper error handling. Handle database query failures, Clerk user lookup failures, and return appropriate HTTP status codes with structured error responses.
successCriteria: Endpoint returns 200 with user data on success
Database errors return 503 with structured error
Auth errors return 401 with clear message
All promises properly awaited and errors caught

steps.description: Add comprehensive error handling to employees endpoint. Wrap database queries in try/catch, handle RBAC permission failures, add input validation with Zod, return proper status codes (200, 403, 503).
successCriteria: Successful queries return 200 with employee data
Permission failures return 403 with clear message
Database failures return 503 with structured error
All database queries use parameterized inputs via Drizzle

steps.description: Add global error handler middleware for Express, uncaughtException handler, unhandledRejection handler. Log all errors with structured context. Ensure server stays alive on non-fatal errors.
successCriteria: Global error handler catches all unhandled Express errors
Process stays alive on handled errors
Uncaught exceptions logged with full stack trace
Unhandled promise rejections logged and don't crash server

steps.description: Create structured logging utility to replace console.log. Support log levels (debug, info, warn, error), add context fields (requestId, userId, endpoint), integrate with error tracking.
successCriteria: Logger exports debug, info, warn, error functions
All log entries include timestamp and level
Error logs include stack traces
Logger is imported and used in at least one fixed file

steps.description: Extract database connection logic into dedicated module with connection pooling config, health check function, graceful shutdown handler. Export singleton connection instance.
successCriteria: Connection pool properly configured with min/max connections
Health check function tests database connectivity
Graceful shutdown closes connections on SIGTERM
Module exports stable singleton instance

history.completionCriteria: All health check endpoints return 200 status. /api/me and /api/employees return successful responses for authenticated users. Zero unhandled promise rejections in server logs for 5 minutes of continuous operation. Database connection remains stable across 100 consecutive API requests.

failedAttempts.constraint: Geofencing validation is completely broken — employees can clock in from anywhere, violating legal time-tracking compliance requirements
failedAttempts.reason: Geofencing validation constraint is NOT resolved. The constraint requires that POST /api/time-entries/clock-in returns 403 when user is >100m from store, but the codebase provided shows NO geofence validation implementation in the time entries route handler. The crawl verification only checks that pages load (which they do), but does NOT verify the actual geofence enforcement behavior. The re-crawl results show WebSocket connection failures and page load issues, but these are separate from the geofencing constraint. Critical: zero of the 6 planned steps have been completed.
